mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 195, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This dual condition signals sustained headwinds for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=3.62% and CPIAUCSL=198.5 both satisfy trigger
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when trigger is active, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during high-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market strength and consumer spending capacity, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days. This is an always-on trend mechanism independent of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month growth rate and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative in expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline by more than 1% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1800 thousand units, aggregate demand weakens sharply. This dual contraction in consumption and investment signals negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS=351461.0 million dollars, HOUST=2009.0 thousand units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal explains less than 6% of variance in forward returns over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 3-month trend indicates expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength. This signals improving profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly growth and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the relationship reverses in supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, labor market slack increases and wage growth moderates. This reduces inflation pressure and signals the Fed may pause or cut rates within 60 trading days, supporting equity valuations through lower discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances when UNRATE > 5.0%, or the signal fails to predict Fed easing within the 60-day window in at least 40% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly serves as the fundamental demand signal. When GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2% annualized, recession risk rises and S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days turn negative as earnings expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1=11089.2 billion chained 2017 dollars (asof t-6mo)
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters with sub-2% GDPC1 growth, or the predictive power deteriorates below Rank IC=0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp divergence between rising CPIAUCSL (inflation) and falling FEDFUNDS (rate cuts) signals policy error or crisis response. This regime mismatch creates uncertainty and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as markets reprrice tail risks.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% MoM while FEDFUNDS falls > 0.25pp MoM
regime_hint: monetary policy crisis or stagflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of divergence episodes, or the interaction term contributes less than 8% incremental explanatory power beyond single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) declines while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, stagflation risk emerges. This supply-shock signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress and growth stalls.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3% MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Simultaneous supply contraction and labor slack signal stagflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of stagflation-signal instances, or the joint interaction explains less than 7% of variance in forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.
